workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Give me the manager view on this Evergreen Solutions support ticket. Need this before my 2pm call.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 08:52 EST
Subject: URGENT: competitive positioning Issue at Evergreen Solutions

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 15:55 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with competitive positioning. What are the next steps from your side?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-C trap (Manager brief reuses customer draft language); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-C failure mode (Manager brief reuses customer draft language)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-C -- Manager brief reuses customer draft language
